activation_results.reason: Input is a near-verbatim restatement of the skill description.
Paraphrase swapping Audit for Review still matches the Dockerfile/compose audit use case.
Explicitly invokes the skill by name.
SSH key forwarding on Windows is unrelated to container auditing.
Meta-question about skills, not a Docker/compose audit request.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: No instances of color/optimize/behavior/organize/center/defense/analyze detected in the narrative prose.

criterion_results.text: Findings cite file:line evidence and are categorised by severity
criterion_results.evidence: Findings tables include Sev and Line columns (e.g. CT-001 CRITICAL Dockerfile:14; CT-002 CRITICAL docker-compose.yml:33; CT-003 HIGH Dockerfile:7).

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: "Executive Summary", "Category Scores"
criterion_results.evidence: Both headings appear explicitly as ## Executive Summary and ## Category Scores.

criterion_results.text: Artefact references the input path or target by name (no placeholder paths)
criterion_results.evidence: Concrete targets named: acme-api, Dockerfile, Dockerfile.worker, docker-compose.yml; no {{placeholder}} tokens present.

judge_result.notes: All four judge criteria are satisfied. The artefact uses Australian/neutral spellings throughout, cites file:line evidence with explicit severity columns in every findings table, contains the canonical Executive Summary and Category Scores headings, and names real targets (acme-api and concrete Dockerfile/compose filenames) rather than placeholders. Activation classification is 5/5 correct against expectations.
